content.title: Advantages of public transport
content.summary: {'A': '政治是选择一种交通方式而不是另一种的主要原因，民主程度越高，越倾向于公共交通，波特兰是一个典型例子，当地压力团体迫使进行了一次关于是否将资金用于轻轨的公投，轻轨方案获胜并取得了巨大成功，随后几年，越来越多的轨道系统被建立，极大地改变了城市的性质。', 'B': '这段话主要讲述了在英国，人们上班的交通时间至少六个世纪以来一直保持稳定，但随着城市扩张，公共基础设施未能跟上步伐，导致交通拥堵问题严重，通勤时间大大增加。', 'C': '尽管人们普遍认为财富的增加会鼓励人们居住在离城市更远的地方，而只有汽车是可行的交通工具，但欧洲城市的例子证明了这一观点的错误。欧洲城市通常比美国的城市更富裕，但汽车使用率却没有达到相同的水平。在斯德哥尔摩，随着城市的扩大和富裕程度的提高，汽车使用率实际上在近年来有所下降。一项新的研究更加明确地证明了这一点。亚洲的发展中城市，如雅加达和曼谷，比东京和新加坡等富裕的亚洲城市更多地使用汽车。在后来发展的城市中，世界银行和亚洲开发银行不鼓励建设公共交通，人们被迫依赖汽车，从而导致了这些城市的交通拥堵问题。', 'D': '这段话主要讲述了纽曼认为关于如何将为汽车建造的城市转变为铁路使用的最佳研究之一是《城市村庄报告》，该报告以墨尔本为例。该报告发现，将所有人都推向市中心并不是最佳方法，而是提倡在数百个地点，主要是铁路站周围创建城市村庄。', 'E': '随着电信业的发展，人们曾经认为人口会更加分散，不再被迫聚集在城市中，然而ISTP团队的研究表明，在经历了几十年的下降后，城市的人口和就业密度在20世纪80年代上升或保持稳定，原因似乎是将从事相关领域工作的人们聚集在一起是有价值的，因为“新世界在很大程度上将依赖于人类创造力，而创造力在人们面对面交流的地方得以蓬勃发展。”'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: In fact, Newman believes the main reason for adopting one sort of transport over another is politics: 'The more democratic the process, the more public transport is favored.' He considers Portland, Oregon, a perfect example of this. Some years ago, federal money was granted to build a new road. However, local pressure groups forced a referendum over whether to spend the money on light rail instead. The rail proposal won and the railway worked spectacularly well. In the years that have followed, more and more rail systems have been put in, dramatically changing the nature of the city. Newman notes that Portland has about the same population as Perth and had a similar population density at the time.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: In the UK, travel times to work had been stable for at least six centuries, with people avoiding situations that required them to spend more than half an hour travelling to work. Trains and cars initially allowed people to live at greater distances without taking longer to reach their destination. However, public infrastructure did not keep pace with urban sprawl, causing massive congestion problems which now make commuting times far higher.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: There is a widespread belief that increasing wealth encourages people to live farther out where cars are the only viable transport. The example of European cities refutes that. They are often wealthier than their American counterparts but have not generated the same level of car use. In Stockholm, car use has actually fallen in recent years as the city has become larger and wealthier. A new study makes this point even more starkly. Developing cities in Asia, such as Jakarta and Bangkok, make more use of the car than wealthy Asian cities such as Tokyo and Singapore. In cities that developed later, the World Bank and Asian Development Bank discouraged the building of public transport and people have been forced to rely on cars - creating the massive traffic jams that characterize those cities.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Newman believes one of the best studies on how cities built for cars might be converted to rail use is The Urban Village report, which used Melbourne as an example. It found that pushing everyone into the city centre was not the best approach. Instead, the proposal advocated the creation of urban villages at hundreds of sites, mostly around railway stations.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: It was once assumed that improvements in telecommunications would lead to more dispersal in the population as people were no longer forced into cities. However, the ISTP team's research demonstrates that the population and job density of cities rose or remained constant in the 1980s after decades of decline. The explanation for this seems to be that it is valuable to place people working in related fields together. 'The new world will largely depend on human creativity, and creativity flourishes where people come together face-to-face.'

groups.title: List of Headings
groups.desc:
Questions 1-5

Reading Passage 1 has five marked paragraphs, A-E.
Choose the correct heading for each section and move it into the gap.

list.text: A successful exercise in people power
The benefits of working together in cities
Higher incomes need not mean more cars
The impact of telecommunications on population distribution
Responding to arguments against public transport

questions.analysis: Section A 主要描述了波特兰市通过民主程序（公投）成功地将联邦资金用于轻轨建设，并且取得了显著的成功。这与小标题 ii: A successful exercise in people power 完全吻合，因为它强调了公众力量的成功运用。

central_sentences.sentence: Some years ago, federal money was granted to build a new road. However, local pressure groups forced a referendum over whether to spend the money on light rail instead. The rail proposal won and the railway worked spectacularly well.
central_sentences.translation: 几年前，联邦政府拨款建造一条新路。然而，当地压力团体强制举行了一次公投，决定是否将这笔钱用于建设轻轨。轻轨提案获胜，而且轻轨系统运行得非常成功。

target_sen.target_sen: The rail proposal won and the railway worked spectacularly well.
The rail proposal won and the railway worked spectacularly well.
However, local pressure groups forced a referendum over whether to spend the money on light rail instead.
However, local pressure groups forced a referendum over whether to spend the money on light rail instead.

questions.analysis: Section B的内容主要讨论了英国的通勤时间问题。文章提到，尽管火车和汽车最初使人们能够在更远的地方居住而不需要花费更多的时间到达目的地，但由于公共基础设施未能跟上城市扩展的步伐，导致了严重的拥堵问题，使得通勤时间大大增加。这与小标题vii: Increases in travelling time直接对应。

central_sentences.sentence: However, public infrastructure did not keep pace with urban sprawl, causing massive congestion problems which now make commuting times far higher.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，公共基础设施建设没有跟上城市扩张的步伐，导致了严重的交通拥堵问题，这使得现在的通勤时间大大增加。

target_sen.target_sen: However, public infrastructure did not keep pace with urban sprawl, causing massive congestion problems which now make commuting times far higher.
In the UK, travel times to work had been stable for at least six centuries, with people avoiding situations that required them to spend more than half an hour travelling to work.
However, public infrastructure did not keep pace with urban sprawl, causing massive congestion problems which now make commuting times far higher.

questions.analysis: 这段话明确指出，尽管欧洲城市的收入增加了，但汽车的使用并没有相应增加，甚至在一些城市如斯德哥尔摩，汽车使用量还减少了。这正好对应了标题“Higher incomes need not mean more cars”（更高的收入未必意味着更多的汽车）。

central_sentences.sentence: There is a widespread belief that increasing wealth encourages people to live farther out where cars are the only viable transport. The example of European cities refutes that. They are often wealthier than their American counterparts but have not generated the same level of car use.
central_sentences.translation: 人们普遍认为，财富的增加会鼓励人们住在更远的地方，那里只有汽车才是可行的交通工具。但欧洲城市的例子反驳了这一观点。这些城市通常比美国城市更富裕，但并没有产生同样水平的汽车使用。

target_sen.target_sen: There is a widespread belief that increasing wealth encourages people to live farther out where cars are the only viable transport.
They are often wealthier than their American counterparts but have not generated the same level of car use.
In Stockholm, car use has actually fallen in recent years as the city has become larger and wealthier.
They are often wealthier than their American counterparts but have not generated the same level of car use.
In Stockholm, car use has actually fallen in recent years as the city has become larger and wealthier.

questions.analysis: Section D 主要讨论了如何通过创建“urban villages”来避免市中心的拥挤。文中提到“pushing everyone into the city centre was not the best approach” 和 “advocated the creation of urban villages at hundreds of sites”，这与选项 i: “Avoiding an overcrowded centre” 是对应的，因为它们都在讲避免将所有人都集中到市中心，从而避免市中心过于拥挤。

central_sentences.sentence: It found that pushing everyone into the city centre was not the best approach. Instead, the proposal advocated the creation of urban villages at hundreds of sites, mostly around railway stations.
central_sentences.translation: 研究发现，将所有人都集中到市中心并不是最好的方法。相反，该提议主张在数百个地点创建城市村落，主要围绕铁路站点。

syn_ary.synonym: pushing everyone into the city centre

target_sen.target_sen: It found that pushing everyone into the city centre was not the best approach.
It found that pushing everyone into the city centre was not the best approach.

questions.analysis: Section E的核心内容是讨论人们在城市中一起工作所带来的好处。文章提到，将从事相关领域工作的人聚集在一起是有价值的，并且创造力在面对面聚集时会蓬勃发展。这些内容直接对应了小标题iii“在城市中一起工作的好处”。

central_sentences.sentence: The explanation for this seems to be that it is valuable to place people working in related fields together. 'The new world will largely depend on human creativity, and creativity flourishes where people come together face-to-face.'
central_sentences.translation: 这种现象的解释似乎是，将从事相关领域工作的人们聚集在一起是很有价值的。"新世界将在很大程度上依赖于人类的创造力，而创造力在人们面对面相聚的地方蓬勃发展。"

target_sen.target_sen: The explanation for this seems to be that it is valuable to place people working in related fields together.

syn_ary.synonym: place people working in related fields together

target_sen.target_sen: The explanation for this seems to be that it is valuable to place people working in related fields together.
'The new world will largely depend on human creativity, and creativity flourishes where people come together face-to-face.'
It was once assumed that improvements in telecommunications would lead to more dispersal in the population as people were no longer forced into cities.
However, the ISTP team's research demonstrates that the population and job density of cities rose or remained constant in the 1980s after decades of decline.

groups.desc:
Questions 6-10

Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 1?
Choose
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information,
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information,
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

questions.title: The ISTP study examined public and private systems in every city of the world.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“every city of the world”表示“世界上的每个城市”，而原文中明确提到研究只涉及了“thirty-seven cities”（37个城市）。这两个信息是矛盾的，因此题干中的陈述是错误的，正确答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: The study compared the proportion of wealth poured into transport by thirty-seven cities around the world.
central_sentences.translation: 这项研究比较了全球37个城市在交通方面投入财富的比例。

target_sen.target_sen: The study compared the proportion of wealth poured into transport by thirty-seven cities around the world.

questions.title: Efficient cities can improve the quality of life for their inhabitants.
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是 TRUE，因为文章中明确提到 "more efficient cities" 可以 "creating a better place to live"，这与题干中的 "Efficient cities can improve the quality of life for their inhabitants" 是同义表达。通过找到这些对应关系，学生可以确定答案为 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Professor Peter Newman, ISTP Director, pointed out that these more efficient cities were able to put the difference into attracting industry and jobs or creating a better place to live.
central_sentences.translation: ISTP主任Peter Newman教授指出，这些更高效的城市能够将节省下来的资金用于吸引产业和就业，或者创造更好的生活环境。

target_sen.target_sen: Professor Peter Newman, ISTP Director, pointed out that these more efficient cities were able to put the difference into attracting industry and jobs or creating a better place to live.

syn_ary.synonym: creating a better place to live

target_sen.target_sen: Professor Peter Newman, ISTP Director, pointed out that these more efficient cities were able to put the difference into attracting industry and jobs or creating a better place to live.

questions.title: An inner-city tram network is dangerous for car drivers.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“An inner-city tram network is dangerous for car drivers”在文章中没有提到。虽然文章提到墨尔本的电车网络和汽车使用量的关系，但并没有提到电车网络对汽车司机是否危险。因此，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.reason: 原文只提电车降低市中心汽车使用率，未涉及安全性。
central_sentences.analysis: 未提及电车对司机是否危险。

questions.title: In Melbourne, people prefer to live in the outer suburbs.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "prefer to live in the outer suburbs" 意思是人们更喜欢住在外郊区，而原文中提到 "explosion in demand for accommodation in the inner suburbs" 表明人们更喜欢住在内城区。这与题干的陈述相反，因此答案是 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: The explosion in demand for accommodation in the inner suburbs of Melbourne suggests a recent change in many people's preferences as to where they live.
central_sentences.translation: 墨尔本内城区住宅需求的激增表明，最近人们对居住地点的偏好发生了变化。

target_sen.target_sen: According to Professor Newman, the larger Australian city of Melbourne is a rather unusual city in this sort of comparison.
Melbourne's large tram network has made car use in the inner city much lower, but the outer suburbs have the same car-based structure as most other Australian cities.
The explosion in demand for accommodation in the inner suburbs of Melbourne suggests a recent change in many people's preferences as to where they live.
Melbourne's large tram network has made car use in the inner city much lower, but the outer suburbs have the same car-based structure as most other Australian cities.
The explosion in demand for accommodation in the inner suburbs of Melbourne suggests a recent change in many people's preferences as to where they live.

questions.title: Cities with high levels of bicycle usage can be efficient even when public transport is only averagely good.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 TRUE，因为文章明确提到阿姆斯特丹和哥本哈根这两个自行车友好城市，即使它们的公共交通系统只是“合理但不特别”，它们仍然非常高效（very efficient）。通过识别题干和文章中的同义表达，学生可以确认这一点。

central_sentences.sentence: Bicycle use was not included in the study but Newman noted that the two most 'bicycle friendly' cities considered - Amsterdam and Copenhagen - were very efficient, even though their public transport systems were 'reasonable but not special'.
central_sentences.translation: 虽然自行车使用并未包含在研究中，但纽曼指出，被考虑的两个最"自行车友好"的城市——阿姆斯特丹和哥本哈根——非常高效，即便它们的公共交通系统只是"合理但并不特别"。

target_sen.target_sen: Bicycle use was not included in the study but Newman noted that the two most 'bicycle friendly' cities considered - Amsterdam and Copenhagen - were very efficient, even though their public transport systems were 'reasonable but not special'.
A new study conducted for the World Bank by Murdoch University's Institute for Science and Technology Policy (ISTP) has demonstrated that public transport is more efficient than cars.
Professor Peter Newman, ISTP Director, pointed out that these more efficient cities were able to put the difference into attracting industry and jobs or creating a better place to live.
Newman, however, believes the study demonstrates that 'the auto-dependent city model is inefficient and grossly inadequate in economic as well as environmental terms'.

Bicycle use was not included in the study but Newman noted that the two most 'bicycle friendly' cities considered - Amsterdam and Copenhagen - were very efficient, even though their public transport systems were 'reasonable but not special'.

syn_ary.synonym: public transport systems were 'reasonable but not special'

target_sen.target_sen: Bicycle use was not included in the study but Newman noted that the two most 'bicycle friendly' cities considered - Amsterdam and Copenhagen - were very efficient, even though their public transport systems were 'reasonable but not special'.

locating_words.locating_word: public transport is only averagely good

groups.title: List of Descriptions
groups.desc:
Questions 11-13
Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.

list.text: successfully uses a light rail transport system in hilly environment
successful public transport system despite cold winters
profitably moved from road to light rail transport system
hilly and inappropriate for rail transport system
heavily dependent on cars despite widespread poverty
inefficient due to a limited public transport system

questions.title: Perth
questions.analysis: 文章中明确提到珀斯市的公共交通系统非常有限（minimal public transport），并且由于这种有限的公共交通系统，交通成本很高（17% of its wealth went into transport costs），这表明其效率低下（inefficient）。因此，选项F“inefficient due to a limited public transport system”是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: The study found that the Western Australian city of Perth is a good example of a city with minimal public transport. As a result, 17% of its wealth went into transport costs.
central_sentences.translation: 研究发现，西澳大利亚的珀斯市是一个公共交通极少的城市的典型例子。结果，该市17%的财富用于交通成本。

target_sen.target_sen: The study found that the Western Australian city of Perth is a good example of a city with minimal public transport.
The study found that the Western Australian city of Perth is a good example of a city with minimal public transport.

questions.title: Auckland
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是D。文章中明确提到Auckland是一个多山的城市，并且很难发展出一个好的铁路网络，这与选项D中的“hilly and inappropriate for rail transport system”完全对应。因此，选项D是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: For example, Newman accepts it would be hard for a city as hilly as Auckland to develop a really good rail network.
central_sentences.translation: 例如，Newman承认，对于像奥克兰这样多山的城市来说，要发展一个真正好的铁路网络是很困难的。

target_sen.target_sen: For example, Newman accepts it would be hard for a city as hilly as Auckland to develop a really good rail network.

questions.title: Portland
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C: profitably moved from road to light rail transport system。文章中明确提到波特兰从公路系统转向轻轨系统，并且这种转变是成功的（"the railway worked spectacularly well"）。题干中的“Portland”与原文中的“Portland, Oregon”对应，“moved from road to light rail”与原文中的“federal money was granted to build a new road. However, local pressure groups forced a referendum over whether to spend the money on light rail instead. The rail proposal won”对应，“successfully”与“the railway worked spectacularly well”对应。

central_sentences.sentence: Some years ago, federal money was granted to build a new road. However, local pressure groups forced a referendum over whether to spend the money on light rail instead. The rail proposal won and the railway worked spectacularly well. In the years that have followed, more and more rail systems have been put in, dramatically changing the nature of the city.
central_sentences.translation: 几年前，联邦政府拨款建设新的公路。然而，当地压力团体迫使举行公投，决定是否将这笔钱用于建设轻轨。轻轨提案获胜，并且轻轨系统运行得非常成功。在随后的几年里，越来越多的轨道系统被建立起来，极大地改变了这座城市的性质。

target_sen.target_sen: He considers Portland, Oregon, a perfect example of this.

content.title: GREYING POPULATION STAYS IN THE PINK

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Elderly people are growing healthier, happier and more independent, say American scientists. The results of a 14-year study to be announced later this month reveal that the diseases associated with old age are afflicting fewer and fewer people and when they do strike, it is much later in life.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: In the last 14 years, the National Long-term Health Care Survey has gathered data on the health and lifestyles of more than 20,000 men and women over 65. Researchers, now analysing the results of data gathered in 1994, say arthritis, high blood pressure and circulation problems—the major medical complaints in this age group—are troubling a smaller proportion every year. And the data confirms that the rate at which these diseases are declining continues to accelerate. Other diseases of old age—dementia, stroke, arteriosclerosis and emphysema—are also troubling fewer and fewer people.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: 'It really raises the question of what should be considered normal ageing,' says Kenneth Manton, a demographer from Duke University in North Carolina. He says the problems doctors accepted as normal in a 65-year-old in 1982 are often not appearing until people are 70 or 75.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Clearly, certain diseases are beating a retreat in the face of medical advances. But there may be other contributing factors. Improvements in childhood nutrition in the first quarter of the twentieth century, for example, gave today's elderly people a better start in life than their predecessors.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: On the downside, the data also reveals failures in public health that have caused surges in some illnesses. An increase in some cancers and bronchitis may reflect changing smoking habits and poorer air quality, say the researchers. 'These may be subtle influences,' says Manton, 'but our subjects have been exposed to worse and worse pollution for over 60 years. It's not surprising we see some effect.'

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: One interesting correlation Manton uncovered is that better-educated people are likely to live longer. For example, 65-year-old women with fewer than eight years of schooling are expected, on average, to live to 82. Those who continued their education live an extra seven years. Although some of this can be attributed to a higher income, Manton believes it is mainly because educated people seek more medical attention.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: The survey also assessed how independent people over 65 were, and again found a striking trend. Almost 80% of those in the 1994 survey could complete everyday activities ranging from eating and dressing unaided to complex tasks such as cooking and managing their finances. That represents a significant drop in the number of disabled old people in the population. If the trends apparent in the United States 14 years ago had continued, researchers calculate there would be an additional one million disabled elderly people in today's population. According to Manton, slowing the trend has saved the United States government's Medicare system more than $200 billion, suggesting that the greying of America's population may prove less of a financial burden than expected.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: The increasing self-reliance of many elderly people is probably linked to a massive increase in the use of simple home medical aids. For instance, the use of raised toilet seats has more than doubled since the start of the study, and the use of bath seats has grown by more than 50%. These developments also bring some health benefits, according to a report from the MacArthur Foundation's research group on successful ageing. The group found that those elderly people who were able to retain a sense of independence were more likely to stay healthy in old age.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: Maintaining a level of daily physical activity may help mental functioning, says Carl Cotman, a neuroscientist at the University of California at Irvine. He found that rats that exercise on a treadmill have raised levels of brain-derived neurotrophic factor coursing through their brains. Cotman believes this hormone, which keeps neurons functioning, may prevent the brains of active humans from deteriorating.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: As part of the same study, Teresa Seeman, a social epidemiologist at the University of Southern California in Los Angeles, found a connection between self-esteem and stress in people over 70. In laboratory simulations of challenging activities such as driving, those who felt in control of their lives pumped out lower levels of stress hormones such as cortisol. Chronically high levels of these hormones have been linked to heart disease.

paragraphs.label: K
paragraphs.text: But independence can have drawbacks. Seeman found that elderly people who felt emotionally isolated maintained higher levels of stress hormones even when asleep. The research suggests that older people fare best when they feel independent but know they can get help when they need it.

paragraphs.label: L
paragraphs.text: 'Like much research into ageing, these results support common sense,' says Seeman. They also show that we may be underestimating the impact of these simple factors. 'The sort of thing that your grandmother always told you turns out to be right on target,' she says.

groups.desc:
Questions 14-22
Complete the summary using the list of words, A-Q below.
Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.
groups.collect: Research carried out by scientists in the United States has shown that the proportion of people over 65 suffering from the most common age-related medical problems is $1923$and that the speed of this change is $1924$. It also seems that these diseases are affecting people$1925$in life than they did in the past. This is largely due to developments in $1926$but other factors such as improved$1927$may also be playing a part. Increases in some other illnesses may be due to changes in personal habits and to $1928$. The research establishes a link between levels of $1929$and life expectancy. It also shows that there has been a considerable reduction in the number of elderly people who are $1930$which means that the$1931$involved in supporting this section of the population may be less than previously predicted.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "proportion of people over 65 suffering from the most common age-related medical problems" 对应文章中的 "the major medical complaints in this age group - are troubling a smaller proportion every year"。因此，最直接的同义表达是 "falling"。

central_sentences.sentence: The results of a 14-year study to be announced later this month reveal that the diseases associated with old age are afflicting fewer and fewer people and when they do strike, it is much later in life.
central_sentences.translation: 这项将于本月晚些时候公布的14年研究结果显示，与老年相关的疾病正影响越来越少的人，即使发病，也比以前更晚。

central_sentences.sentence: Researchers, now analysing the results of data gathered in 1994, say arthritis, high blood pressure and circulation problems - the major medical complaints in this age group - are troubling a smaller proportion every year.
central_sentences.translation: 研究人员在分析1994年收集的数据结果后表示，关节炎、高血压和循环系统问题——这个年龄组的主要医疗问题——每年影响的人群比例都在减少。

target_sen.target_sen: Researchers, now analysing the results of data gathered in 1994, say arthritis, high blood pressure and circulation problems - the major medical complaints in this age group - are troubling a smaller proportion every year.

central_sentences.sentence: And the data confirms that the rate at which these diseases are declining continues to accelerate.

central_sentences.sentence: The results of a 14-year study to be announced later this month reveal that the diseases associated with old age are afflicting fewer and fewer people and when they do strike, it is much later in life.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“developments in 【 】”对应原文中的“medical advances”。“advances”与“developments”是同义词，而“medical”对应的选项是“medicine”。因此，正确答案是“medicine”。

central_sentences.sentence: Clearly, certain diseases are beating a retreat in the face of medical advances.
central_sentences.translation: 显然，某些疾病在医学进步的推动下正在退却。

target_sen.target_sen: Clearly, certain diseases are beating a retreat in the face of medical advances.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "developments" 对应原文中的 "advances"，而 "improved" 对应原文中的 "improvements"。在原文中，"Improvements in childhood nutrition" 提到营养的改善是一个重要因素，因此答案是 "nutrition"。

central_sentences.sentence: Clearly, certain diseases are beating a retreat in the face of medical advances. But there may be other contributing factors. Improvements in childhood nutrition in the first quarter of the twentieth century, for example, gave today's elderly people a better start in life than their predecessors.
central_sentences.translation: 显然，某些疾病在医学进步的推动下正在逐步减少。但可能还有其他因素在发挥作用。例如，20世纪前四分之一世纪儿童营养的改善，使得当今的老年人比他们的前辈有了更好的人生起点。

target_sen.target_sen: Clearly, certain diseases are beating a retreat in the face of medical advances.
Improvements in childhood nutrition in the first quarter of the twentieth century, for example, gave today's elderly people a better start in life than their predecessors.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“increases in some other illnesses”对应原文中的“surges in some illnesses”，“changes in personal habits”对应“changing smoking habits”。原文中提到“poorer air quality”导致了疾病的增加，并进一步解释为“pollution”。因此，正确答案是“pollution”。

central_sentences.sentence: An increase in some cancers and bronchitis may reflect changing smoking habits and poorer air quality, say the researchers.
central_sentences.translation: 研究人员表示，某些癌症和支气管炎的增加可能反映了吸烟习惯的改变和空气质量的恶化。

questions.analysis: 题干中的“levels”和“life expectancy”在原文中分别对应“levels”和“live longer”。原文明确指出了受教育程度（education）与预期寿命（life expectancy）之间的联系，因此正确答案是“education”。

central_sentences.sentence: One interesting correlation Manton uncovered is that better-educated people are likely to live longer.
central_sentences.translation: Manton发现了一个有趣的相关性，即受教育程度较高的人更可能长寿。

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "considerable reduction" 对应原文中的 "significant drop"，而 "elderly people who are" 对应 "disabled old people"。因此，正确答案是 "disabled"。题干和原文中的关键表达直接对应，学生可以通过识别同义表达和关键单词来正确选择答案。

central_sentences.sentence: That represents a significant drop in the number of disabled old people in the population.
central_sentences.translation: 这表明人口中残疾老年人的数量显著下降。

central_sentences.sentence: According to Manton, slowing the trend has saved the United States government's Medicare system more than $200 billion, suggesting that the greying of America's population may prove less of a financial burden than expected.
central_sentences.translation: 根据Manton的说法，这一趋势的放缓为美国政府的医疗保险系统节省了超过2000亿美元，这表明美国人口老龄化可能带来的财政负担比预期的要小。

groups.desc:
Questions 23-26
Complete each sentence with the correct ending, A-H, below.
Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.

list.text: can be helped by hormone treatment.
may cause rises in levels of stress hormones.
have cost the United States government more than $200 billion.
allow old people to be more independent.
can reduce stress in difficult situations.

questions.title: Home medical aids
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是G：“allow old people to be more independent”。文章中提到“许多老年人的自立能力增加可能与简单家庭医疗辅助设备的使用大幅增加有关”，这里的“self-reliance”即“独立性”，与题干中的“independent”直接对应。因此，家庭医疗辅助设备使老年人更加独立。

central_sentences.sentence: The increasing self-reliance of many elderly people is probably linked to a massive increase in the use of simple home medical aids.
central_sentences.translation: 许多老年人日益增强的独立性可能与大量使用简单的家庭医疗辅助设备有关。

target_sen.target_sen: The increasing self-reliance of many elderly people is probably linked to a massive increase in the use of simple home medical aids.

questions.title: Regular amounts of exercise
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Regular amounts of exercise”在原文中对应的是“Maintaining a level of daily physical activity”。原文中提到这种活动可能有助于心理功能的运作，并且进一步解释了这种活动可能会防止大脑退化。因此，正确答案是E: may help prevent mental decline。

central_sentences.sentence: Maintaining a level of daily physical activity may help mental functioning, says Carl Cotman, a neuroscientist at the University of California at Irvine. He found that rats that exercise on a treadmill have raised levels of brain-derived neurotrophic factor coursing through their brains. Cotman believes this hormone, which keeps neurons functioning, may prevent the brains of active humans from deteriorating.
central_sentences.translation: 加州大学欧文分校的神经科学家Carl Cotman说，保持每日一定水平的体力活动可能有助于维持大脑功能。他发现在跑步机上运动的老鼠体内脑源性神经营养因子的水平升高。Cotman认为，这种能够保持神经元功能的激素可能会防止活跃的人类大脑退化。

syn_ary.synonym: Maintaining a level of daily physical activity

target_sen.target_sen: Maintaining a level of daily physical activity may help mental functioning, says Carl Cotman, a neuroscientist at the University of California at Irvine.

questions.title: Feelings of control over life
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 H: "can reduce stress in difficult situations"。题干中的“control over life”在原文中对应的是“felt in control of their lives”，而“reduce stress”对应的是“lower levels of stress hormones”。实验情境中的“challenging activities such as driving”就是“difficult situations”。因此，答案 H 最为合适，因为它最直接对应了文章中的关键信息：对生活的控制感在困难情境下能够降低应激激素水平，减少压力。

central_sentences.sentence: In laboratory simulations of challenging activities such as driving, those who felt in control of their lives pumped out lower levels of stress hormones such as cortisol.
central_sentences.translation: 在模拟具有挑战性的活动（如驾驶）的实验中，那些感觉自己能掌控生活的人分泌的应激激素（如皮质醇）水平较低。

syn_ary.synonym: felt in control of their lives

target_sen.target_sen: In laboratory simulations of challenging activities such as driving, those who felt in control of their lives pumped out lower levels of stress hormones such as cortisol.

questions.title: Feelings of loneliness
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Feelings of loneliness" 对应原文中的 "emotionally isolated"，而 "may cause rises in levels of stress hormones" 对应原文中的 "maintained higher levels of stress hormones"。因此，正确答案是 C。

central_sentences.sentence: Seeman found that elderly people who felt emotionally isolated maintained higher levels of stress hormones even when asleep.
central_sentences.translation: Seeman发现，即使在睡眠时，那些感到情感孤立的老年人也会维持较高水平的压力激素。

target_sen.target_sen: Seeman found that elderly people who felt emotionally isolated maintained higher levels of stress hormones even when asleep.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: One of the first great intellectual feats of a young child is learning how to talk, closely followed by learning how to count. From earliest childhood we are so bound up with our system of numeration that it is a feat of imagination to consider the problems faced by early humans who had not yet developed this facility. Careful consideration of our system of numeration leads to the conviction that, rather than being a facility that comes naturally to a person, it is one of the great and remarkable achievements of the human race.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: It is impossible to learn the sequence of events that led to our developing the concept of number. Even the earliest of tribes had a system of numeration that, if not advanced, was sufficient for the tasks that they had to perform. Our ancestors had little use for actual numbers; instead their considerations would have been more of the kind Is this enough? rather than How many? when they were engaged in food gathering, for example. However, when early humans first began to reflect on the nature of things around them, they discovered that they needed an idea of number simply to keep their thoughts in order. As they began to settle, grow plants and herd animals, the need for a sophisticated number system became paramount. It will never be known how and when this numeration ability developed, but it is certain that numeration was well developed by the time humans had formed even semi-permanent settlements.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Evidence of early stages of arithmetic and numeration can be readily found. The indigenous peoples of Tasmania were only able to count one, two, many; those of South Africa counted one, two, two and one, two twos, two twos and one, and so on. But in real situations the number and words are often accompanied by gestures to help resolve any confusion. For example, when using the one, two, many type of system, the word many would mean, Look at my hands and see how many fingers I am showing you. This basic approach is limited in the range of numbers that it can express, but this range will generally suffice when dealing with the simpler aspects of human existence.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The lack of ability of some cultures to deal with large numbers is not really surprising. European languages, when traced back to their earlier version, are very poor in number words and expressions. The ancient Gothic word for ten, tachund, is used to express the number 100 as tachund tachund. By the seventh century, the word teon had become interchangeable with the tachund or hund of the Anglo-Saxon language, and so 100 was denoted as hund teontig, or ten times ten. The average person in the seventh century in Europe was not as familiar with numbers as we are today. In fact, to qualify as a witness in a court of law a man had to be able to count to nine!

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Perhaps the most fundamental step in developing a sense of number is not the ability to count, but rather to see that a number is really an abstract idea instead of a simple attachment to a group of particular objects. It must have been within the grasp of the earliest humans to conceive that four birds are distinct from two birds; however, it is not an elementary step to associate the number 4, as connected with four birds, to the number 4, as connected with four rocks. Associating a number as one of the qualities of a specific object is a great hindrance to the development of a true number sense. When the number 4 can be registered in the mind as a specific word, independent of the object being referenced, the individual is ready to take the first step toward the development of a notational system for numbers and, from there, to arithmetic.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Traces of the very first stages in the development of numeration can be seen in several living languages today. The numeration system of the Tsimshian language in British Columbia contains seven distinct sets of words for numbers according to the class of the item being counted: for counting flat objects and animals, for round objects and time, for people, for long objects and trees, for canoes, for measures, and for counting when no particular object is being numerated. It seems that the last is a later development while the first six groups show the relics of an older system. This diversity of number names can also be found in some widely used languages such as Japanese.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Intermixed with the development of a number sense is the development of an ability to count. Counting is not directly related to the formation of a number concept because it is possible to count by matching the items being counted against a group of pebbles, grains of corn, or the counter's fingers. These aids would have been indispensable to very early people who would have found the process impossible without some form of mechanical aid. Such aids, while different, are still used even by the most educated in today's society due to their convenience. All counting ultimately involves reference to something other than the things being counted. At first it may have been grains or pebbles but now it is a memorised sequence of words that happen to be the names of the numbers.

groups.desc:
Questions 27-31
Complete each sentence with the correct ending, A-G, below.
Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.

list.text: was necessary in order to fulfil a civic role.
was necessary when people began farming.
was necessary for the development of arithmetic.
was used when the range of number words was restricted.
can be traced back to early European languages.
was a characteristic of early numeration systems.

questions.title: A developed system of numbering
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B，因为文章中明确提到，当人们开始定居、种植植物和放牧动物时，对复杂数字系统的需求变得至关重要。这与题干中的“developed system of numbering”以及选项B中的“when people began farming”相对应。

central_sentences.sentence: As they began to settle, grow plants and herd animals, the need for a sophisticated number system became paramount.
central_sentences.translation: 当他们开始定居、种植作物和放牧动物时，对复杂的数字系统的需求变得至关重要。

target_sen.target_sen: As they began to settle, grow plants and herd animals, the need for a sophisticated number system became paramount.

questions.title: An additional hand signal
questions.analysis: 题干中的“hand signal”在原文中对应的是“gestures”，而“restricted”对应的是“limited”。原文提到，当数字词汇有限时，手势被用来帮助表达数字。因此，正确答案是E。

central_sentences.sentence: But in real situations the number and words are often accompanied by gestures to help resolve any confusion.
central_sentences.translation: 但在实际情况下，数字和词语经常伴随着手势，以帮助解决任何混淆。

target_sen.target_sen: But in real situations the number and words are often accompanied by gestures to help resolve any confusion.

questions.title: In seventh-century Europe, the ability to count to a certain number
questions.analysis: 在七世纪的欧洲，一个人必须能够数到九才能够作为法庭的证人。这表明数到某个特定数字是履行公民职责（如在法庭上作证）的必要条件。因此，正确答案是 A: was necessary in order to fulfil a civic role。

central_sentences.sentence: In fact, to qualify as a witness in a court of law a man had to be able to count to nine!
central_sentences.translation: 事实上，要在法庭上获得作为证人的资格，一个人必须能够数到九！

target_sen.target_sen: The average person in the seventh century in Europe was not as familiar with numbers as we are today.
In fact, to qualify as a witness in a court of law a man had to be able to count to nine!

locating_words.locating_word: ability to count to a certain number

questions.title: Thinking about numbers as concepts separate from physical objects
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Thinking about numbers as concepts separate from physical objects”在文章中对应的表达是“see that a number is really an abstract idea instead of a simple attachment to a group of particular objects”。文章进一步解释了这种抽象概念对于算术发展的重要性，因此正确答案是C: was necessary for the development of arithmetic。

central_sentences.sentence: Perhaps the most fundamental step in developing a sense of number is not the ability to count, but rather to see that a number is really an abstract idea instead of a simple attachment to a group of particular objects.
central_sentences.translation: 在发展数字概念方面，最基本的步骤可能不是数数的能力，而是认识到数字实际上是一个抽象的概念，而不是简单地附着在一组特定物体上。

target_sen.target_sen: Perhaps the most fundamental step in developing a sense of number is not the ability to count, but rather to see that a number is really an abstract idea instead of a simple attachment to a group of particular objects.

It must have been within the grasp of the earliest humans to conceive that four birds are distinct from two birds; however, it is not an elementary step to associate the number 4, as connected with four birds, to the number 4, as connected with four rocks.
Associating a number as one of the qualities of a specific object is a great hindrance to the development of a true number sense.

When the number 4 can be registered in the mind as a specific word, independent of the object being referenced, the individual is ready to take the first step toward the development of a notational system for numbers and, from there, to arithmetic.

Perhaps the most fundamental step in developing a sense of number is not the ability to count, but rather to see that a number is really an abstract idea instead of a simple attachment to a group of particular objects.

Perhaps the most fundamental step in developing a sense of number is not the ability to count, but rather to see that a number is really an abstract idea instead of a simple attachment to a group of particular objects.

Perhaps the most fundamental step in developing a sense of number is not the ability to count, but rather to see that a number is really an abstract idea instead of a simple attachment to a group of particular objects.

questions.title: Expressing number differently according to class of item
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Expressing number differently according to class of item”在文章中对应的句子是“seven distinct sets of words for numbers according to the class of the item being counted”。这表明不同类别的物品有不同的数字表达方式，这是早期计数系统的一个特征。因此，正确答案是G: was a characteristic of early numeration systems。

central_sentences.sentence: The numeration system of the Tsimshian language in British Columbia contains seven distinct sets of words for numbers according to the class of the item being counted: for counting flat objects and animals, for round objects and time, for people, for long objects and trees, for canoes, for measures, and for counting when no particular object is being numerated.
central_sentences.translation: 不列颠哥伦比亚省的齐姆沙语数字系统包含七组不同的数字词，根据所计数物品的类别而定：用于计数扁平物体和动物、圆形物体和时间、人、长形物体和树木、独木舟、度量单位，以及在不计数特定物体时使用的数字词。

syn_ary.synonym: seven distinct sets of words for numbers

target_sen.target_sen: The numeration system of the Tsimshian language in British Columbia contains seven distinct sets of words for numbers according to the class of the item being counted: for counting flat objects and animals, for round objects and time, for people, for long objects and trees, for canoes, for measures, and for counting when no particular object is being numerated.

syn_ary.synonym: class of the item being counted

target_sen.target_sen: The numeration system of the Tsimshian language in British Columbia contains seven distinct sets of words for numbers according to the class of the item being counted: for counting flat objects and animals, for round objects and time, for people, for long objects and trees, for canoes, for measures, and for counting when no particular object is being numerated.

groups.desc:
Questions 32-40

Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 3?
Choose
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information,
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information,
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

questions.title: For the earliest tribes, the concept of sufficiency was more important than the concept of quantity.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“sufficiency”对应文章中的“enough”，“quantity”对应文章中的“How many”。文章明确指出，早期人类更多考虑的是“够不够”，而不是“有多少”，与题干的意思一致，因此答案是TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Our ancestors had little use for actual numbers; instead their considerations would have been more of the kind Is this enough? rather than How many?
central_sentences.translation: 我们的祖先很少使用实际的数字；相反，他们的考虑更多的是"这够了吗？"而不是"有多少？"

target_sen.target_sen: Our ancestors had little use for actual numbers; instead their considerations would have been more of the kind Is this enough?
rather than How many?
Our ancestors had little use for actual numbers; instead their considerations would have been more of the kind Is this enough?

questions.title: Indigenous Tasmanians used only four terms to indicate numbers of objects.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Indigenous Tasmanians" 对应原文中的 "The indigenous peoples of Tasmania"，题干中的 "four terms" 对应原文中的 "one, two, many"。从原文可以看出，塔斯马尼亚原住民只使用了三个术语来表示数量：one, two, many，而不是四个术语。因此，题干中的信息与文章内容不符，正确答案是 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: The indigenous peoples of Tasmania were only able to count one, two, many;
central_sentences.translation: 塔斯马尼亚的原住民只能数到一、二、多；

target_sen.target_sen: The indigenous peoples of Tasmania were only able to count one, two, many; those of South Africa counted one, two, two and one, two twos, two twos and one, and so on.
The indigenous peoples of Tasmania were only able to count one, two, many; those of South Africa counted one, two, two and one, two twos, two twos and one, and so on.

questions.title: Some peoples with simple number systems use body language to prevent misunderstanding of expressions of number.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "simple number systems" 对应文章中的 "one, two, many type of system"；"body language" 对应 "Look at my hands and see how many fingers I am showing you"；"prevent misunderstanding" 对应 "to help resolve any confusion"。这些同义替换词在文章中明确指出了使用肢体语言来防止数字表达的误解。因此，答案是 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: For example, when using the one, two, many type of system, the word many would mean, Look at my hands and see how many fingers I am showing you.
central_sentences.translation: 例如，当使用"一、二、多"这种系统时，"多"这个词的意思就是"看看我的手，看我给你展示几根手指"。

syn_ary.synonym: one, two, many type of system

target_sen.target_sen: For example, when using the one, two, many type of system, the word many would mean, Look at my hands and see how many fingers I am showing you.

syn_ary.synonym: Look at my hands and see how many fingers I am showing you

target_sen.target_sen: For example, when using the one, two, many type of system, the word many would mean, Look at my hands and see how many fingers I am showing you.
But in real situations the number and words are often accompanied by gestures to help resolve any confusion.

questions.title: All cultures have been able to express large numbers clearly.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "All cultures have been able to express large numbers clearly" 表示所有文化都能够清晰地表达大数字。而原文中明确提到 "The lack of ability of some cultures to deal with large numbers"，即一些文化缺乏处理大数字的能力。这与题干的陈述是相反的，因此答案是 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: The lack of ability of some cultures to deal with large numbers is not really surprising.
central_sentences.translation: 一些文化缺乏处理大数字的能力并不令人惊讶。

target_sen.target_sen: The lack of ability of some cultures to deal with large numbers is not really surprising.
The lack of ability of some cultures to deal with large numbers is not really surprising.

questions.title: The word 'thousand' has Anglo-Saxon origins.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词“thousand”在原文中并没有直接提到。原文提到的是“tachund”和“hund”，并且解释了它们在古哥特语和盎格鲁-撒克逊语中的用法，但没有提到“thousand”这个词的起源。因此，题干中的信息在文章中找不到，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.reason: display_answer=NG；原文未出现“thousand”及其词源信息，无法判断真伪。
central_sentences.analysis: 文中只谈100的词源演变，未提thousand。

questions.title: In general, people in seventh-century Europe had poor counting ability.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 TRUE，因为文章明确指出七世纪欧洲人对数字不熟悉，甚至只需要能数到九就能成为法庭证人，这表明他们的计数能力确实很差。通过识别题干中的关键词 "seventh-century Europe" 和 "poor counting ability"，并在文章中找到对应的同义替换词 "the seventh century in Europe" 和 "not as familiar with numbers" 以及 "count to nine"，可以确定答案句所在的位置，从而得出正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: The average person in the seventh century in Europe was not as familiar with numbers as we are today. In fact, to qualify as a witness in a court of law a man had to be able to count to nine!
central_sentences.translation: 在七世纪的欧洲，普通人对数字的熟悉程度远不如我们今天。事实上，要想在法庭上成为一名合格的证人，一个人只需要能数到九就行了！

target_sen.target_sen: The average person in the seventh century in Europe was not as familiar with numbers as we are today.
The average person in the seventh century in Europe was not as familiar with numbers as we are today.

questions.title: In the Tsimshian language, the number for long objects and canoes is expressed with the same word.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词“long objects”和“canoes”在原文中明确提到是用不同的词汇来表示数字的。原文中提到“for long objects and trees”和“for canoes”是不同的类别，说明它们不是用同一个词汇来表达。因此，题干中的说法是错误的，正确答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: The numeration system of the Tsimshian language in British Columbia contains seven distinct sets of words for numbers according to the class of the item being counted: for counting flat objects and animals, for round objects and time, for people, for long objects and trees, for canoes, for measures, and for counting when no particular object is being numerated.
central_sentences.translation: 在不列颠哥伦比亚省，Tsimshian语言的计数系统包含七套不同的数字词汇，这些词汇根据所计数物品的类别而不同：用于计数扁平物体和动物、圆形物体和时间、人、长物体和树木、独木舟、度量单位，以及当没有特定物体被计数时使用的词汇。

target_sen.target_sen: The numeration system of the Tsimshian language in British Columbia contains seven distinct sets of words for numbers according to the class of the item being counted: for counting flat objects and animals, for round objects and time, for people, for long objects and trees, for canoes, for measures, and for counting when no particular object is being numerated.

The numeration system of the Tsimshian language in British Columbia contains seven distinct sets of words for numbers according to the class of the item being counted: for counting flat objects and animals, for round objects and time, for people, for long objects and trees, for canoes, for measures, and for counting when no particular object is being numerated.

The numeration system of the Tsimshian language in British Columbia contains seven distinct sets of words for numbers according to the class of the item being counted: for counting flat objects and animals, for round objects and time, for people, for long objects and trees, for canoes, for measures, and for counting when no particular object is being numerated.

questions.title: The Tsimshian language contains both older and newer systems of counting.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Tsimshian language" 和 "older and newer systems" 在原文中找到了对应的表达 "Tsimshian language" 和 "older system" 以及 "later development"。这些词汇表明了Tsimshian语言中存在旧的和新的计数系统。因此，答案是 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: It seems that the last is a later development while the first six groups show the relics of an older system.
central_sentences.translation: 似乎最后一组是较晚的发展，而前六组显示了旧系统的遗迹。

target_sen.target_sen: The numeration system of the Tsimshian language in British Columbia contains seven distinct sets of words for numbers according to the class of the item being counted: for counting flat objects and animals, for round objects and time, for people, for long objects and trees, for canoes, for measures, and for counting when no particular object is being numerated.
It seems that the last is a later development while the first six groups show the relics of an older system.
It seems that the last is a later development while the first six groups show the relics of an older system.

questions.title: Early peoples found it easier to count by using their fingers rather than a group of pebbles.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“早期人类发现用手指比用小石子更容易数数”在原文中没有明确提到。原文提到早期人类可以用手指、小石子等辅助工具来数数，但没有比较哪种方式更容易。因此，正确答案是 NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: These aids would have been indispensable to very early people who would have found the process impossible without some form of mechanical aid.
central_sentences.translation: 对于早期人类来说，这些辅助工具是不可或缺的，没有某种形式的机械辅助，他们会发现这个过程是不可能的。

central_sentences.sentence: Counting is not directly related to the formation of a number concept because it is possible to count by matching the items being counted against a group of pebbles, grains of corn, or the counter's fingers.
central_sentences.translation: 计数与数字概念的形成并不直接相关，因为可以通过将被计数的物品与一堆小石子、玉米粒或计数者的手指进行匹配来进行计数。

target_sen.target_sen: These aids would have been indispensable to very early people who would have found the process impossible without some form of mechanical aid.
These aids would have been indispensable to very early people who would have found the process impossible without some form of mechanical aid.

Counting is not directly related to the formation of a number concept because it is possible to count by matching the items being counted against a group of pebbles, grains of corn, or the counter's fingers.

Counting is not directly related to the formation of a number concept because it is possible to count by matching the items being counted against a group of pebbles, grains of corn, or the counter's fingers.

Counting is not directly related to the formation of a number concept because it is possible to count by matching the items being counted against a group of pebbles, grains of corn, or the counter's fingers.
At first it may have been grains or pebbles but now it is a memorised sequence of words that happen to be the names of the numbers.

At first it may have been grains or pebbles but now it is a memorised sequence of words that happen to be the names of the numbers.
